disease_name: Hyper-IgE syndrome 6, autosomal dominant, with recurrent infections

definition_population.content: AD-HIES (Job syndrome, STAT3-HIES) is a rare inborn error of immunity from dominant-negative STAT3 mutations causing recurrent infections, eczema, and elevated IgE.

sources.quote: The hyper-IgE syndromes (HIES) are a heterogeneous group of inborn errors of immunity sharing manifestations including increased infection susceptibility, eczema, and raised serum IgE.

epidemiology.content: No specific prevalence/incidence figure was found; disease is rare and studied via case series/cohorts accumulated since original description 55 years prior to a 2021 review.

sources.quote: Since the prototypical HIES description 55 years ago, areas of significant progress have included description of key disease-causing genes

biology.content: Dominant-negative STAT3 mutations produce broad innate/acquired immune defects plus skeletal/connective-tissue/vascular abnormalities; pathophysiology implicates JAK-STAT signaling, IL-6/IL-17, and Th17 cells; phenocopies occur via IL6ST and ZNF341 mutations in the same pathway.

sources.quote: conferring a broad immune defect across both innate and acquired arms, as well as defects in skeletal, connective tissue, and vascular function
driven understanding of the interface of several signaling pathways, including the JAK-STAT pathways, interleukins 6 and 17, and the role of Th17 lymphocytes
identification of phenocopies such as mutations in IL6ST and ZNF341

treatment.content: No FDA-approved disease-specific therapy identified in this pass. Historical management relies on antimicrobial prophylaxis; early allogeneic stem cell transplant attempts were not positive.

sources.quote: treatment has hinged on aggressive antimicrobial prophylaxis and treatment to prevent the development of end-organ disease such as pneumatocele

regulatory_trials.content: ClinicalTrials.gov search for 'hyper-IgE syndrome' returned 17 total trial records (registries/infection-management focus); no STAT3-targeted interventional trial identified in this pass.

gaps: No STAT3-targeted drug or trial identified
No FDA drug regulatory record specific to this indication found
